channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The US dominated figure skating's world championships. It may face the Russians at the Olympics
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — It's hard to imagine a better world championships for the American figure skating team, which captured gold in three of the four disciplines over the weekend for the first time in its history, and will take all of that momentum into an Olympic year.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sears, Alabama can't duplicate record 3-point night, fall to Duke with Final Four at stake
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Mark Sears shot a free throw late in the second half that could have cut Alabama's deficit to 10 — a last-gasp effort at a comeback still within reach — and watched the ball clang off the back rim. Even the supposed gimmes weren’t automatic for Sears on this night.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USC shakes off JuJu Watkins' absence and beats Kansas State 67-61 in March Madness
item.description: SPOKANE, Ore. (AP) — Freshman Kennedy Smith stepped into the void left by injured star JuJu Watkins, scoring 19 points to help Southern California hold off Kansas State 67-61 in the Sweet 16 of the women's NCAA Tournament on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Sox DH Rafael Devers is 1st big leaguer with 10 strikeouts in 3 games to open a season
item.description: Boston Red Sox designated hitter Rafael Devers has become the first major league player to strike out 10 times in the first three games of a season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Madison Booker and top-seeded Texas outlast SEC rival Tennessee 67-59 to reach Elite Eight
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Texas coach Vic Schaefer got the tense battle he expected between his defensive-minded Longhorns and the highest-scoring team in the country in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No longer sick, Dodgers' Mookie Betts hits a pair of '160-pound homers' as he works to gain weight
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Mookie Betts is no longer ailing, finally able to keep food down and eating up a storm to pack on the weight he dropped while battling a stomach virus during the first two weeks of the season. And, as the eight-time All-Star reminded everyone, “I'm still good at baseball.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fans chant 'sell the team' at Rays home opener; team owner says talks are nothing new
item.description: The Rays pulled out a win in their new home for the season at Steinbrenner Field, but the ball club's future in Tampa Bay remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 9th-inning homer lifts Rays over Rockies 3-2 in Steinbrenner Field opener
item.description: Rookie Kameron Misner led off the ninth inning with his first major league home run, giving Tampa Bay a 3-2 win over the Colorado Rockies on Friday as the Rays began their season of home games at Steinbrenner Field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CF Montreal seeks first win of the season, visits the Chicago Fire
item.description: CF Montreal (0-4-1) vs. Chicago Fire (3-1-1) Chicago; Saturday, 8:30 p.m. EDT BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Chicago -175, Montreal +429, Draw +308; over/under is 2.5 goals BOTTOM LINE: CF Montreal seeks its first victory of the season when it visits the Chicago Fire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DePaul women's basketball coach Doug Bruno steps down after 39 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Chicago has produced a lot of basketball icons and one of them in Lincoln Park is stepping down — DePaul's longtime women's basketball coach Doug Bruno.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: RSL seeks redemption in Minnesota after home setback
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4) — Real Salt Lake looks to bounce back from a rare home loss as they go on the road to face Minnesota United FC on Saturday night. Real Salt Lake’s attack will be bolstered by the return of Polish attacking winger Dominic Marzcuk and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Lightning strike early and often in 8-0 win over Utah HC
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club will need close to a miracle to make the NHL playoffs now. After suffering its worst blowout loss of the season, coupled with a St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Sox fans shower Derrick Rose with MVP chants during first pitch
item.description: CHICAGO - Coming back to Chicago never gets old for Derrick Rose. The former Bulls MVP returned to the South Side Thursday to throw out the first pitch at the White Sox home opener, where he was showered with MVP chants from the crowd. "It's surreal. It's always surreal.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox hammer Angels in lopsided Opening Day win
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The weather on White Sox Opening Day mirrored their franchise outlook heading into 2025. Murky, gray and dreary with little to be excited about besides the fact baseball season is all the way back. Good thing the White Sox played well in spite of the melancholy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: Cubs have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox Opening Day: A lot of 'new' on the South Side in 2025. What to know if you're headed to Rate Field
item.description: CHICAGO — After a 2024 season that everyone would rather forget, there's a lot of "new" on the South Side as the White Sox get set to embark on their 2025 campaign Thursday. There's a new manager, Will Venable. There's a new stadium name, Rate Field. There's a new food and beverage menu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Florida coach brings team to famed local staple ahead of March Madness matchup in SF
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Florida head coach Todd Golden is excited to be back in San Francisco this week -- a city where he began his head coaching career. Golden leads the No. 1 seed Gators for their Sweet 16 matchup Thursday against No. 4 seed Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: White Sox DFA Oscar Colás, claim switch-hitting outfielder off waivers from Rockies
item.description: The Chicago White Sox designated top prospect Oscar Colás for assignment and claimed switch-hitting outfielder Greg Jones off waivers from the Colorado Rockies, while reassigned five players to minor league camp and optioned right-handed pitcher Justin Anderson to Triple-A.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Softball: Poway 2, Patrick Henry 0
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- A gorgeous day to play softball on Monday afternoon with Patrick Henry hosting Poway. The Titans won a fantastic game 2-0 in 10 innings. Great pitching on both sides with Mya McGowan leading the way for Poway with a complete game shutout and adding 12 strikeouts.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Major League Cricket coming to Oakland Coliseum
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- In the East Bay, the City of Oakland has announced that Major League Cricket will play matches at the Coliseum this summer. Cricket is the world’s second most popular sport, after soccer.
item.dc:creator: Charles Clifford, KRON4

item.title: Tony Finau out to win second Houston Open
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Utah native Tony Finau came into this year after going through two pretty big life events. The first was knee surgery back in December.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: USC star JuJu Watkins suffers season-ending knee injury in March Madness win over Mississippi State
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins was carried off the floor with a season-ending knee injury in the first quarter of top-seeded Southern California’s game against Mississippi State in the NCAA Tournament on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mitchell returns to Utah, leads Cavs past Jazz
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Jarrett Allen scored 18 points and Donovan Mitchell added 16, helping Cleveland snap a four-game losing streak with a 120-91 victory over the Utah Jazz on Sunday. Evan Mobley had 11 points, 11 rebounds and three blocks to help the Cavaliers get back in the win column.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: New York girls basketball coach fired for hair-pulling incident with player apologizes
item.description: In a statement shared with local media, Zullo, 81, said he regretted his behavior and offered apologies to the player, her family, the team, the district, and the community.
item.dc:creator: Griffin Haas, NewsNation
